rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, Line 21, Defendant Millie Luong'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint Overruled. Defendant demurs pursuant to CCP sec 430.10(f) and (g). The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") is not ambiguous as to what was agreed to in a written contract and what was agreed to orally. (See e.g. TAC, 72.) rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER DUNN et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Denied. (CCP § 437c(f)(1).) Defendant CCSF moves for summary adjudication of parts of a cause of action that will not dispose of the entire negligence cause of action. See Notice of Motion, p. 1:27 2:1; Complaint, 10(a), (e) and (f).) This is not permitted by statute that is the basis for the motion. See CCP §437c(f)(1) ("A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action . ¿"). The allegations pertaining to entrustment, training and supervision are not separate and distinct wrongful acts giving rise to separate causes of action. The authority cited by Defendant is distinguishable. Edward Fineman Co. v. Superior Court (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 1110, 1118 involved summary adjudication that claims as to 23 out of 83 checks were time barred, because each was a "separate and distinct wrongful act" and because "notwithstanding the aggregation of the 23 1991-1993 checks, each constitutes a separate cause of action." Similarly, Lilienthal & Fowler v. Sup.Ct. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848, 1851, 1854, cited by Defendant in reply, permitted summary adjudication in a legal malpractice action where plaintiffs "sought to recover damages based on two separate and distinct obligations" combined in the same cause of action that had "no relation to each other and involve legal services performed at different times, with different and distinct obligations and distinct and separate alleged damages." Plaintiffs here have multiple legal theories, but they pertain to a single primary right. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: NEGINA MOHAMMAD VS. LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with 10 days leave to amend to allege, if Plaintiff can in good faith, that the manufacturer of the dough sheeter "designed, installed, or otherwise required by specification for the attachment of guards and conveyed knowledge of the same to the employer," as required by Labor Code §4558(c). (See also Award Metals Inc. v. Superior Court (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 1128, 1134.) The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") adequately pleads facts establishing that a reasonable person could find that the dough sheeter falls under the definition of "Power Press" in Lab. Code sec. 4558. (TAC, 6a; See Islas v. D&G Manufacturing Company, Inc. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 571, holding where there are two reasonable interpretations of whether a machine is a "power press" under Labor Code sec. 4558, it is a question of fact for the jury to decide; see also Rosales v. Deputy Ace Medical Company (2000) 22 Cal.4th 279, 286, holding that a power press is a machine that utilizes "the use of a tool that imparts shape to material by pressing or impacting against or through the material, that is, by punching, stamping, or extruding.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CIP AIRPORT INDUSTRIAL/FLEX PORTFOLIO LLC, VS. WILLIAM R. HAMBRECHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Judgment Debtor'S Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFF CIP AIRPORT INDUSTRIAL/FLEX PORTFOLIO LLC'S Motion For Order Compelling Judgment Debtor's Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Judgment Debtor shall produce all of the documents that are subject to the motion to compel. Judgment Debtor may designate documents as confidential pursuant to a stipulated protective order that meets the requirements of California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551. The stipulated protective order proposed by Judgment Debtor appears to be modeled on one used by Judgment Debtor in federal proceedings and does not comport with California law. It creates, contrary to the holding of NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TB), Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, a blanket presumption of confidentiality without the specific findings that California Rule of Court 2.550 requires the court to make prior to allowing evidence to be filed under seal. The parties shall, prior to the hearing, meet and confer and then at the hearing present the court with a stipulated protective order. The proposed order must place the burden on the party claiming that documents qualify for protection to move the court to allow the documents to be filed under seal, and must contain provisions complying with the requirements of California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551. The parties can find model stipulated protective orders that meet these requirements on the Complex Litigation Page of the San Mateo County Superior Court and on the Los Angeles Superior Court's Complex Court Informational Materials page. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK WALLACE VS. CARLOS JESUS VILLANUEVA, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Attend Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT CARLOS VILLANUEVA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Attend Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff to provide responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatories - Set One is GRANTED. Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff to provide response to Defendant's Requests for Production of Documents - Set One is DENIED. Defendant has not provided the Court with Attachment "A" to Defendant's Request for Production of Documents that contains the actual document requests themselves. Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff to attend his deposition is GRANTED. Within 21 days of service of notice of entry of order on Plaintiff, Plaintiff shall provide verified responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatories - Set One; Plaintiff shall submit to a deposition lasting no more than 7 hours at a location chosen by Defendant in the City and County of San Francisco within 30 days of notice of entry of this order, and shall produce at his deposition the documents identified in Exhibit "A" to the Notice of Deposition of Plaintiff previously served on September 9, 2013. Plaintiff shall, within 30 days of notice of entry of this order pay Defendant sanctions in the amount of $460 to compensate Defendant for the two hours of attorney time required to prepare the motion to compel and the $60 filing fee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ULRIKE SILKEY et al VS. ROBERT BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Ulrike Silkey'S And David Silkey'S Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For An Order For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, line 5. Plaintiffs Ulrike Silkey And David Silkey's Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For An Order For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED AS MOOT. Defendant's initial response to Form Interrogatory 15.1, consisting solely of objections, was improper and constitutes a misuse of the discovery process. A party may not evade its responsibility to respond to Form Interrogatory 15.1 with facts simply by asserting that its investigation and discovery are not complete. Nevertheless, Defendant has supplemented its responses after the filing of Plaintiffs' motion, and Plaintiffs' reply brief does not challenge the adequacy of the supplemental responses. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM MOHAMMED VS. LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled in part and sustained in part with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. As to the third cause of action, sustained with leave to amend as to Plaintiff Ibrahim Mohammed ("Mohammed") to allege facts that support that he is a third party beneficiary; overruled as to Plaintiff Mi Pueblito Market, LLC ("LLC"). As to first and second causes of action as to Mohammed, sustained with leave to amend for the same grounds as to the third cause of action; as to LLC, sustained with leave to amend to plead the first and second causes of action as a single non-duplicative cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. As to the fourth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as Plaintiffs have withdrawn the cause of action. As to the fifth and seventh causes of action, Plaintiffs are given 10 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to plead additional facts to meet the particularity standard for a cause of action of fraud. (See Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645 ("how, when, where, to whom, and by what means").) As to the sixth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as exemplary damages are a remedy, not a cause of action, and the facts supporting exemplary damages should be pled in the charging allegations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS VS. TAYLOR K. SMITH MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT The motion is continued to January 22, 2014, for Judge Goldsmith to hear the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ROCKY POINT PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. YAT-PANG AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANTS YAT-PANG AU, RUSSIAN HILL GROUP, LLC, NOB HILL VISTAS, LLC, EUREKA PARK, LLC, YAT-HON AU, YAT-GAI AU AND PARNASSUS PLACE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm
